title: 10 best herbal teas for indigestion

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yellow gentian tea contains flavonoids and other anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort.

Digestive enzymes stimulation: The bitter compounds in yellow gentian tea stimulate the production of digestive enzymes in the stomach and intestines, which helps to break down food more efficiently and relieve symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and gas.

Spasmolytic properties: Yellow gentian tea has been shown to have spasmolytic properties, meaning it can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can contribute to indigestion and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gentiana lutea, also known as yellow gentian, teas helps with indigestion because of its bitter compounds, which stimulate digestive enzymes and enhance gut motility. The tea's bitter properties also increase bile production, aiding in the breakdown and absorption of fats. Additionally, Gentiana lutea has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with indigestion. This makes it a popular herbal remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated stomach linings and reduce inflammation that may contribute to indigestion symptoms.

Iridoid glycosides: Gentiana lutea teas contain iridoid glycosides that have been shown to have anti-spasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce cramping and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Gentiana lutea teas have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce oxidative stress that may contribute to indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Gentiana lutea are commonly used to make teas for indigestion because they contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds.
Leaves: The leaves are used in teas for indigestion due to their ability to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation.
Roots: The roots are also used for their carminative properties, helping to relieve bloating and gas associated with indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of dried gentiana lutea roots from a trusted supplier or forage responsibly in the wild.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at 150-200 degrees fahrenheit for 1-2 hours to reduce moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for 2-3 minutes.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the ground root powder with 8 ounces of boiling water to make the tea.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes and strain the tea before serving to enjoy its earthy flavor.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Angelica tea helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to indigestion and discomfort.
Carminative: The tea's carminative properties help to alleviate gas and bloating by preventing the formation of gas bubbles and promoting their release from the body.
Anti-inflammatory: Angelica tea's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to indigestion, nausea, and abdominal pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica, also known as angelica, teas helps with indigestion because of its carminative properties. The volatile oils present in the plant, such as angelic acid and ligustilide, have a soothing effect on the digestive system. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother digestion and relieving symptoms of indigestion, including bloating and cramps. Regular consumption of angelica tea may also stimulate digestive enzymes and improve overall gut health.
remedy_constituents: Coumarins: These compounds help alleviate indigestion by reducing inflammation and relaxing smooth muscle in the digestive tract, allowing for more efficient digestion.

Phenolic acids (including ferulic acid and sinapic acid): These antioxidants help protect the digestive tract from oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and improve gut health, all of which contribute to alleviating indigestion symptoms.
Apiol: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part for making teas for indigestion as they contain a high concentration of angelic root extract, which has carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas for indigestion due to their ability to ease stomach discomfort and reduce inflammation.
Stems: The stems are also used to make teas for indigestion as they have a similar composition to the roots and can help soothe digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest angelica roots and rhizomes in late autumn or early spring when they are at their peak potency.
Wash the harvested roots and rhizomes with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned roots and rhizomes into small pieces and dry them immediately using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Steep one teaspoon of dried angelica root and rhizome in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids before serving in hot or iced form.

remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and coats the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with indigestion.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion like bloating and cramps.

Digestive enzymes inhibitors: Marshmallow tea has been shown to inhibit certain digestive enzymes that can contribute to indigestion, allowing for better digestion and reducing symptoms of discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, teas helps with indigestion because of its soothing properties and mucilaginous compounds. The mucilage in marshmallow root coats and protects the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause indigestion. Additionally, the antispasmodic properties of Althaea officinalis help to calm muscle spasms in the digestive tract, allowing food to pass through more smoothly and reducing discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate indigestion and promote digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing of mucous membranes, alleviating indigestion symptoms.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviate pain, and promote a healthy gut environment.

Mucilages (primarily galactomannans): These water-soluble compounds form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing irritation and inflammation in the digestive tract, and providing relief from indigestion and heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines.
Leaves: They are used because they contain a range of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in relieving digestive discomfort.
Stems: They are used for similar reasons as the roots and leaves, as they also contain mucilages that can help to calm digestive issues and promote a sense of comfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 ounces of dried althaea roots or 4-6 grams of fresh althaea roots.
Rinse the harvested althaea roots with filtered water to remove impurities.
Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried althaea roots or 1-2 tablespoons of fresh althaea roots.
Infuse the althaea roots in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the herbal tea and discard the solids to enjoy a soothing cup.

remedy_properties: Carvone content: Caraway tea contains a high concentration of carvone, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe digestive tract muscles and reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines.

Antioxidant activity: Caraway tea is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, common factors contributing to indigestion.

Choleretic properties: Caraway tea has been shown to stimulate bile production and release, which helps improve digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating, cramping, and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Carum carvi, also known as caraway, teas helps with indigestion because of its carvone and limonene compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut health. It has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and stomach discomfort, by reducing inflammation and enhancing digestion. Additionally, the tea's anti-spasmodic properties help to calm the muscles in the digestive tract, promoting a smoother and more efficient digestive process. remedy_constituents: Carvone: Carvone, a monoterpene ketone, helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract, promoting smooth muscle relaxation and alleviating symptoms of indigestion.
Limonene: Limonene, a terpene, aids in indigestion relief by enhancing digestion, reducing inflammation, and alleviating symptoms of nausea and vomiting.

Coumarin: Coumarin, a phenolic compound, helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation and promoting the relaxation of smooth muscles in the digestive tract, which can ease symptoms of indigestion and digestive discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used for their carminative properties, which help to ease digestion and relieve indigestion.
Roots: They are used for their warming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe digestive discomfort and reduce indigestion.
Rhyzomes: They are used for their carminative and anti-spasmodic properties, which help to relieve gas, bloating, and other digestive issues associated with indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather carum carvi seeds and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 1 hour.
Grind 2 teaspoons of dried carum carvi seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of powdered carum carvi in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth into a cup to remove the solids from the liquid.
Serve the tea immediately and enjoy its warm aroma and herbal flavor.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe digestive issues such as indigestion, by reducing inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining.

Gastroprotective compounds: Chamomile contains gastroprotective compounds that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, gastritis, and other digestive issues, thus alleviating symptoms of indigestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with indigestion because of its soothing and calming properties. Additionally, chamomile's natural relaxant properties can help to calm the muscles in the stomach and reduce spasms, allowing the digestive system to function more efficiently and effectively. remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues.
Leaves: They are utilized for their bitter compounds that aid in stimulating digestion and relieving indigestion symptoms.
Seeds: They are used due to their ability to aid in digestion and relieve indigestion symptoms, although they are less commonly used than flowers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated allowing the flowers to retain their potency.
Rinse 1-2 teaspoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with cold water to remove any impurities.
Steep the dried flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable temperature before consumption taking about 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Berberine: Has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with indigestion.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried roots of hydrastis canadensis in quantities of one teaspoon per cup.
Combine the roots with boiling water in a ratio of 1:8 to 1:10.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and steep for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool before serving and consuming immediately.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel tea's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps associated with indigestion.

Phenolic compounds (specifically, myristicin): Phenolic compounds, such as myristicin, found in Foeniculum vulgare teas help with indigestion by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, ultimately alleviating indigestion symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and leaves in a quantity of 1-2 teaspoons per 8 oz of water.
Heat the water to a temperature of 212 degrees fahrenheit in a glass teapot for 5 minutes.
Steep the foeniculum vulgare mixture in the hot water for 5-7 minutes to allow flavors to infuse.
Strain the tea through a fine mesh sieve to remove foeniculum vulgare solids and discard the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature for 10-15 minutes before serving.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried plantago major leaves and flowers for a standard tea batch.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried herbs with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes or until the tea reaches your desired strength.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 4-6 dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer or early fall.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra leaves and flowers in quantities of one ounce per quart of boiling water.
Combine the herb mixture with boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot or infuser for five to seven minutes of steeping time.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose particles and sediment.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves per 8 ounces water.
Combine measured herbs with boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes or according to package instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with indigestion because its active compound, gingerol, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. As a natural carminative, it helps to relieve bloating and discomfort by stimulating digestion and improving gut motility. Additionally, ginger's warming properties help to ease cramps and spasms in the stomach, providing rapid relief from indigestion symptoms such as heartburn and nausea. Gastric Motility Enhancer: Ginger's active compound, gingerol, stimulates the movement of food through the stomach and intestines, reducing symptoms of bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with indigestion.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 inches of fresh ginger root and clean it thoroughly under running water.
Peel the ginger root using a vegetable peeler and cut it into thin slices about 1/8 inch thick.
Combine one teaspoon of sliced ginger with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining the tea into a cup.
Enjoy your soothing zingiber officinale tea warm or at room temperature within the next 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried echinacea purpurea flowers into a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Boil one cup of fresh water and pour it over the measured echinacea purpurea flowers in the cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup or teapot to remove the solids and preserve the flavor.
Drink the echinacea purpurea tea hot, as soon as possible after preparation, to help alleviate indigestion symptoms within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with indigestion because of its natural diuretic properties, which help to stimulate digestion and alleviate bloating. The tea's bitter compounds also work to tone and regulate the digestive system, reducing inflammation and soothing irritated mucous membranes. Additionally, dandelion's prebiotic fibers feed beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy balance in the gut microbiome. This combination of actions helps to calm indigestion symptoms, such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort, allowing for more efficient digestion and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Bitter compounds: Dandelion tea contains bitter compounds like taraxinic acid, which stimulate digestive enzymes and improve digestion by increasing saliva production, helping to alleviate indigestion symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion tea, such as sesquiterpenes, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to indigestion and other digestive issues.

Stimulant laxative effects: Dandelion tea contains inulin, a prebiotic fiber that stimulates the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, helping to regulate bowel movements and alleviate constipation, a common symptom of indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots have a bitter and earthy flavor, making them ideal for digestive issues as they stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion.
Fruits (Achenes): Dandelion fruits, also known as achenes, contain bioactive compounds that help soothe stomach irritation and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers with cold water to remove impurities.
Combine one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale herb with boiling water to create a tea infusion.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to dissolve and blend.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup to remove solids.
Drink the tea immediately, while it is still warm, to soothe indigestion symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cumin teas helps with indigestion because of its unique properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The antioxidants present in cumin tea help to reduce inflammation in the stomach, alleviating discomfort and bloating associated with indigestion. Additionally, cumin's natural carminative properties help to expel gas from the body, providing rapid relief from cramps and discomfort. As a result, cumin teas can offer a natural solution for individuals experiencing recurring episodes of indigestion and other digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Cumin tea helps to eliminate gas and reduce bloating, which can alleviate discomfort and cramps associated with indigestion.
Anti-inflammatory property: Cumin tea has anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which may contribute to indigestion symptoms like pain and cramping.
Antispasmodic property: Cumin tea can help relax the muscles of the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate indigestion symptoms like nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils, the roots help soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, the barks can aid in reducing stomach acidity and alleviating symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 teaspoons of fresh cilantro leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried cilantro powder.
Combine the harvested cilantro with 8 ounces of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes then strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to sweeten the tea and stir well before serving.
Consume the tea immediately after preparation and repeat every 2-3 hours as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with indigestion because of its natural digestive properties. The menthol in peppermint tea relaxes the muscles in the stomach, reducing symptoms such as bloating and cramping. It also helps to increase the flow of bile, which aids in digestion and reduces inflammation in the stomach lining. Additionally, peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the digestive tract, providing relief from discomfort and indigestion. By drinking peppermint tea after meals, you can experience a smoother digestion process and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Peppermint tea's high menthol content helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, easing symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort.

Carminative properties: The essential oils present in peppermint tea, including menthol, limonene, and eucalyptol, have a carminative effect, which means they help to expel gas from the digestive system and relieve symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and flatulence.

Antispasmodic properties: The volatile oils in peppermint tea, including menthol and limonene, have an antispasmodic effect on the muscles of the digestive tract, which helps to reduce spasms and cramps that can occur due to indigestion, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain compounds that help to soothe and calm digestive issues, making them a popular choice for teas intended to alleviate indigestion.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, also known as the underground stem, is rich in menthol and other volatile oils that can help to ease digestive discomfort and reduce symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves or purchase dried peppermint leaves in sufficient quantity.
Measure one teaspoon of dried peppermint leaves or three tablespoons of fresh leaves per cup.
Heat water to a boiling point and then reduce the heat to a simmer for five minutes.
Add measured peppermint leaves to the hot water and steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and serve immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram teas helps with indigestion because its warm, soothing properties ease digestive discomfort and calm stomach upset. The herb's natural carminative qualities help to relieve bloating and gas, while its anti-inflammatory effects reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Additionally, sweet marjoram tea's gentle flavor and aroma can promote relaxation, which can help to alleviate stress-induced indigestion. By sipping on this calming tea, individuals can find relief from discomfort and promote a healthy gut.
remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Stimulant: Marjoram tea contains compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes, helping to break down food and relieve symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort.
Anti-Inflammatory Agent: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by indigestion, heartburn, and acid reflux.
Gastric Soother: The tea's gentle, calming effects on the stomach muscles help to relax and reduce spasms, cramps, and discomfort associated with indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sweet marjoram roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps, due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Marjoram flowers are commonly used in herbal teas to soothe digestive discomforts like indigestion and gas, thanks to their gentle carminative and anti-spasmodic effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried origanum majorana leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for one cup of tea.
Measure out one teaspoon of the herb for every eight ounces of boiling water and place it in a heat-resistant teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the herb and allow it to steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose particles or debris.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before serving, enjoying warm or at room temperature as needed to alleviate indigestion symptoms.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to indigestion that are treated with herbal teas are heartburn, bloating, and acid reflux. Ginger tea, in particular, is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the stomach and alleviate these symptoms. Other herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and licorice root also have calming effects on the digestive system, making them effective natural remedies for these discomforts.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for indigestion are a blend of plant-based ingredients that help alleviate digestive discomfort by soothing the stomach and promoting relaxation. The best herbal teas for indigestion include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Cinchona officinalis, Matricaria chamomilla, Mentha x piperita, Peumus boldus, Ginkgo biloba, Taraxacum officinale, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, indigestion can lead to chronic pain, weight loss, fatigue, and anxiety, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with indigestion is a blend of peppermint, ginger, and chamomile. Peppermint tea aids in digestion and relaxes the muscles in the stomach, while ginger tea helps to reduce inflammation and soothe nausea. Chamomile tea calms the mind and body, reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can exacerbate indigestion. Together, these teas provide a soothing and effective solution for alleviating indigestion and promoting overall digestive health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Explore (New York, N.Y.), the researchers used a mix of two herbal medicines, Zataria-Trachyspermum (ZT), to investigate its effect on the clinical symptoms of patients with Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS). The study involved 50 patients who were assigned to one of three parallel groups: the ZT capsule group, the placebo group, and the mebeverine capsule group. The results showed that after the fourth week of intervention, symptoms of pain, bloating, and reflux significantly decreased in the ZT group compared to the placebo and mebeverine groups (P < 0.05), indicating that herbal teas, in this case, ZT, may be beneficial for indigestion by reducing symptoms of pain, bloating, and reflux.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas helps with indigestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, gingerol, soothes the digestive tract and relaxes the muscles that line the stomach. This reduces inflammation and alleviates symptoms such as bloating, nausea, and cramps. Additionally, ginger has a calming effect on the digestive system, helping to regulate digestive enzymes and improve nutrient absorption. Regular consumption of Zingiber officinale teas may also promote a balanced gut microbiome, further contributing to its digestive benefits.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help alleviate indigestion by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother digestion.
Shogaols: Shogaols help combat indigestion by inhibiting the production of gastric acid and enzymes that cause irritation in the digestive system, reducing discomfort and pain.
Zingiberene: Zingiberene acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale, rhyzomes contain compounds that help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing indigestion and discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves of Zingiber officinale are often used in combination with the rhyzome, providing additional digestive benefits and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe indigestion.
Buds: Zingiber officinale buds are sometimes used to make teas, offering a milder flavor and similar digestive benefits to the rhyzome, with a focus on calming the stomach and reducing nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale roots by carefully digging them up from your garden or a nearby field.
Clean the roots by gently scrubbing them with a soft brush to remove any dirt particles.
Slice the zingiber officinale roots into thin pieces using a sharp knife or a root slicer.
Steep one teaspoon of sliced zingiber officinale roots in one cup of boiling water for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea by pouring it through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the root pieces.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas helps with indigestion because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds like glycyrrhizin, which have a calming effect on the stomach and alleviate symptoms of indigestion. Additionally, licorice root tea has been shown to enhance the production of stomach mucus, protecting the stomach lining from acid damage and promoting a healthy digestive environment, thus providing relief from indigestion and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, providing relief from irritation and discomfort.
Flavonoids: They help with indigestion by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which can help protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals.
Saponins: They help with indigestion by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and soothing agent, which can help reduce digestive discomfort and promote a healthy gut environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are widely used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps soothe digestive issues.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are also used to make teas, as they contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help calm the digestive system.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are sometimes used to make teas, as they contain similar compounds to the roots and leaves, which can help alleviate indigestion and other digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and place it in a heat-resistant container.
Boil 1 liter of water for 5-7 minutes to reach a temperature of 95-100 degrees celsius.
Add the dried root to the boiling water and simmer for 5-10 minutes to infuse the liquid.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids from the tea.
Allow the tea to cool for 2-3 minutes before serving and consuming the herbal infusion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas helps with indigestion because it contains a compound called anethole, which has natural carminative properties. Anethole helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort. Additionally, Foeniculum vulgare tea has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to indigestion. By calming the digestive system and reducing inflammation, Foeniculum vulgare tea provides relief from indigestion and promotes a sense of overall digestive well-being.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds help alleviate indigestion by reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining from acid damage.
Anethole: As a carminative agent, anethole helps to alleviate indigestion by releasing gas and reducing bloating.
Furanocoumarins: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has carminative properties that help relieve indigestion.
Leaves: Used for their mild flavor and aromatic properties, which help soothe digestive issues and ease indigestion symptoms.

(Optional) Stems: Although not as commonly used as the seeds or leaves, the stems of Foeniculum vulgare may be used to make teas for indigestion due to their mild flavor and potential digestive benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 teaspoons of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds or roots from a reputable source.
Boil 1 liter of water in a large pot for 5-10 minutes over medium heat.
Add the dried foeniculum vulgare to the boiling water and steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup or teapot.
Drink the warm tea immediately or let it cool and refrigerate for later consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas helps with indigestion because it contains compounds like quinine and alkaloids that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort. Additionally, the tea's astringent properties help to calm the stomach and promote digestion, allowing the body to process food more efficiently. This can lead to a reduction in symptoms of indigestion and an overall improvement in digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Helps to alleviate indigestion by reducing inflammation and pain associated with digestive issues.
Quinidine: Exhibits antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive discomfort and prevent spasms.
Cinchonidine: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive cramps associated with indigestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas due to their high alkaloid content, particularly quinine, which helps in reducing indigestion and inflammation.
Barks: The barks are used for their bitter compounds, which aid in stimulating digestion and relieving indigestion.
Seeds: They contain volatile oils that help to calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of indigestion and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis bark by cutting it into smaller pieces with pruning shears in late summer or early fall.
Dry cinchona officinalis bark in a low-temperature dehydrator at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind 2-3 teaspoons of dried cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Steep the powder in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release the medicinal properties.
Strain the tea and drink immediately or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas helps with indigestion because of its soothing and calming properties. The tea contains apigenin and luteolin, which are flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and cramps. Additionally, chamomile has a natural relaxant effect on the muscles in the digestive system, promoting smooth digestion and reducing discomfort. This makes it an effective natural remedy for indigestion and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, promoting relaxation and soothing the digestive system.

Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and discomfort.

Matricaria chamomilla flavonoids (including apigenin and luteolin): These flavonoids have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting overall gut health and reducing symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds and soothing properties, which help to calm the digestive system and alleviate indigestion symptoms.
Leaves: Although often overlooked, the leaves of Matricaria chamomilla also contain soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for treating indigestion.

Seeds: The seeds of Matricaria chamomilla contain a compound called apigenin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe the digestive system and relieve indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers for brewing.
Steep the dried flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes or the fresh flowers for 2 to 3 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot.
Add honey or lemon to taste and stir well to dissolve the sweetener or release the citrus flavor.
Drink the tea immediately while warm or store it in the refrigerator for later consumption within 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas helps with indigestion because it contains peppermint oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract and calm stomach muscles. This can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating, cramping, and nausea. Additionally, the menthol in peppermint oil can help relax the muscles in the stomach and esophagus, allowing food to pass more easily and reducing discomfort. This can lead to a significant reduction in indigestion symptoms and promote a sense of relief and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with indigestion by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of cramps and spasms.
Menthone: Contributes to indigestion relief by reducing stomach contractions and soothing the mucous membranes, which can help calm heartburn and nausea.

Rosmarinic acid: Provides indigestion relief by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of IBS (Irritable Bowel Syndrome).
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide a soothing effect on the digestive system.
Stems: The stems are also used due to their ability to aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, such as bloating and cramps.
Buds: The buds are used for their mild flavor and ability to ease digestive discomfort, making them an alternative to leaves for those who prefer a less potent tea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a handful of fresh leaves and flowers from mature mentha x piperita plants in the morning.
Rinse the harvested leaves and flowers with cool water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the leaves and flowers into smaller pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter for easier infusion.
Steep one teaspoon of chopped mentha x piperita in a cup of boiling water for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to enjoy the refreshing and invigorating herbal tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peumus boldus teas helps with indigestion because of its unique combination of flavonoids and tannins, which have a soothing effect on the digestive system. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the stomach and intestines, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria. Additionally, Peumus boldus has been traditionally used to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve digestion, making it a natural remedy for relieving indigestion and promoting a comfortable and balanced digestive system. remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These compounds help to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate indigestion symptoms.
Phenolic acids: They exhibit antioxidant properties and can protect the stomach lining from damage, thus reducing indigestion.
Terpenes: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with indigestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Peumus boldus are commonly used to make teas for indigestion due to their high concentration of antioxidants and bitter compounds.
Barks: The barks of Peumus boldus are used to make teas for indigestion as they contain various compounds that can help alleviate digestive issues and stimulate digestion.
Stems: The stems of Peumus boldus are used to make teas for indigestion due to their ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried peumus boldus leaves for every 8 ounces of water required.
Heat 8 ounces of water in a tea infuser or a saucepan over low heat for 5 minutes.
Steep the measured peumus boldus leaves in the heated water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.
Serve the herbal peumus boldus tea warm or at room temperature immediately after straining.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas helps with indigestion because it promotes healthy digestion and relieves symptoms of bloating and cramps. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the tea's ability to improve blood flow to the digestive system aids in the efficient absorption of nutrients and the elimination of waste products, reducing the discomfort associated with indigestion. This results in a faster recovery and improved overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help alleviate indigestion by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the digestive system.
Terpenoids (specifically bilobalide): Soothe the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can lead to indigestion, promoting a more comfortable digestive experience.
Bilobalane: May help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive system, contributing to the alleviation of indigestion symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of the active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, which help to alleviate indigestion symptoms.
Seeds: They contain unique compounds that may help to soothe indigestion and improve digestion.
Barks: They are another source of active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, which may aid in reducing inflammation and indigestion symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water for the tea.
Steep the dried leaves in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine mesh sieve to remove the ginkgo biloba leaves.
Mix 1 tablespoon of honey with the tea to add sweetness and improve flavor.
Serve the ginkgo biloba tea immediately and consume within 30 minutes to 1 hour for maximum benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas helps with indigestion because of its soothing properties and ability to ease digestive discomfort. The dandelion root and leaves used in the tea are rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that promotes the growth of beneficial gut bacteria. This leads to a balanced gut microbiome, which is essential for proper digestion and nutrient absorption. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from indigestion and other digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of indigestion by inhibiting the release of histamine and other digestive irritants.
Saponins: They have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating and discomfort.
Phenolic acids: They have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help to protect the digestive system from damage and alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as nausea and abdominal pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make teas for indigestion because they contain taraxasterol and other saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make teas for indigestion because they have anti-inflammatory and carminative properties that help soothe digestive issues.
Roots: Roots are used to make teas for indigestion because they contain inulin, a prebiotic that helps support gut health and reduce digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in equal amounts approximately 2 grams each.
Combine the gathered taraxacum officinale with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the taraxacum officinale mixture to steep for 5 to 7 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the taraxacum officinale liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Store the taraxacum officinale tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas helps with indigestion because it contains compounds with natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive system. The tea's calming effects can also reduce stress and anxiety, which are often triggers for indigestion. Additionally, the tea's antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, preventing cramps and discomfort associated with indigestion. This can lead to a more comfortable and peaceful digestive process, providing relief from indigestion symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as an anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic agent, helping to reduce inflammation and relieve cramps in the digestive system.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits calming effects on the digestive system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to indigestion.
Rosmarinic acid: Demonstrates antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, protecting the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for teas related to indigestion because they contain calming properties that help soothe stomach issues.
Flowers: Used for teas related to indigestion because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce digestive discomfort.

Stems: Not typically used for teas related to indigestion, however, I will include it, because it has been reported in some sources that stems of Lavandula angustifolia have been used, though it is not a preferred part.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers from a clean and well-maintained source.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the dried flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add honey to taste to the tea based on individual preference for sweetness.
Serve the tea immediately and consume within 30 minutes for optimal flavor and benefits.
